syntax_3321.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3321.title: Border Stabilize
syntax_3321.description: Called when a border (optionally only of the specified world(s)) stops moving.

en: Called when a border (optionally only of the specified world(s)) stops moving.

syntax_3322.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3322.title: Custom Generator
syntax_3322.description:
Not an actual event, but rather an event-level scope used to code a custom generator, with the specified ID. Under the main "event" line you can have three different sub-scopes that handle generation:
initiation: This is called once at the beginning of a world's generation. This is specifically intended for optionally setting the world's spawn, but can also be used for other things you need to do at the start of generation.
generation: This is called whenever a chunk is being generated. All custom generators should use this to do their main generation of the world, using the given chunkdata to set the materials of the blocks in the chunk. Note that no block information beyond the material can be set during this stage. Any block state necessary beyond the material should be set during the population stage.
population: This is called after a chunk and all adjacent chunks (not including ones that are diagonal) have been generated, to add certain blocks on top which may require (or be made easier) by being able to put blocks on more than one chunk (ex. adding flowers). This stage should also be used if you need to specify blockdata in addition to the material.

en:
Not an actual event, but rather an event-level scope used to code a custom generator, with the specified ID. Under the main "event" line you can have three different sub-scopes that handle generation:
initiation: This is called once at the beginning of a world's generation. This is specifically intended for optionally setting the world's spawn, but can also be used for other things you need to do at the start of generation.
generation: This is called whenever a chunk is being generated. All custom generators should use this to do their main generation of the world, using the given chunkdata to set the materials of the blocks in the chunk. Note that no block information beyond the material can be set during this stage. Any block state necessary beyond the material should be set during the population stage.
population: This is called after a chunk and all adjacent chunks (not including ones that are diagonal) have been generated, to add certain blocks on top which may require (or be made easier) by being able to put blocks on more than one chunk (ex. adding flowers). This stage should also be used if you need to specify blockdata in addition to the material.

syntax_3323.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3323.title: Border is Moving
syntax_3323.description: Checks whether the border of the specified world is moving or stable (not moving).

en: Checks whether the border of the specified world is moving or stable (not moving).

syntax_3324.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3324.title: Is Beyond Border
syntax_3324.description: Checks whether a location/entity is beyond or within the border in its world.

en: Checks whether a location/entity is beyond or within the border in its world.

syntax_3325.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3325.title: Chunk is Loaded
syntax_3325.description: Checks whether the specified chunk is loaded.

en: Checks whether the specified chunk is loaded.

syntax_3326.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3326.title: Chunk is Slimey
syntax_3326.description: Checks whether the specified chunk is slimey.

en: Checks whether the specified chunk is slimey.

syntax_3327.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3327.title: Change Border Diameter
syntax_3327.description: Changes the diameter, or size, of the specified world's border over the specified timespan.
syntax_3327.syntax_pattern:
set (diameter|side length|size) of %world% to %number% over %timespan%
set %world%'s (diameter|side length|size) to %number% over %timespan%
set (diameter|side length|size) of %world% over %timespan% to %number%
set %world%'s (diameter|side length|size) over %timespan% to %number%
add %number% to (diameter|side length|size) of %world% over %timespan%
add %number% to %world%'s (diameter|side length|size) over %timespan%
(remove|subtract) %number% from (diameter|side length|size) of %world% over %timespan%
(remove|subtract) %number% from %world%'s (diameter|side length|size) over %timespan%

en: Changes the diameter, or size, of the specified world's border over the specified timespan.

syntax_3328.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3328.title: Reset World Border
syntax_3328.description: Resets the border properties of the specified world to their default values. These values can be found at https://minecraft.gamepedia.com/World_border#Commands

en: Resets the border properties of the specified world to their default values. These values can be found at https://minecraft.gamepedia.com/World_border#Commands

syntax_3329.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3329.title: Load Chunk
syntax_3329.description: Loads or unloads the specified chunk.

en: Loads or unloads the specified chunk.

syntax_3330.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3330.title: Fill Region in ChunkData
syntax_3330.description: Fills a region in the specified chunkdata with the specified material:1. Fills a region between two coordinates in the specified chunkdata2. Fills the specified layer of the specified chunkdata3. Fills the layers from the first to the second specified layer in the specified chunkdata
syntax_3330.syntax_pattern:
fill region from %number%, %number%, %number% to %number%, %number%, %number% in %chunkdata% with %material%
fill layer %number% in %chunkdata% with %material%
fill layers %number% to %number% in %chunkdata% with %material%

en: Fills a region in the specified chunkdata with the specified material:1. Fills a region between two coordinates in the specified chunkdata2. Fills the specified layer of the specified chunkdata3. Fills the layers from the first to the second specified layer in the specified chunkdata

syntax_3331.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3331.title: Create World using Creator
syntax_3331.description: Creates a world using the specified creator, optionally specifying the world's name (this is required if the creator doesn't specify a name).Optionally you can specify that the world should be automatically loaded on server start. See the creator expressions for more information on how to specify the world's name and other settings. If a world with the name (specified or from the creator) already exists, this will just load that world instead of creating a new one.

en: Creates a world using the specified creator, optionally specifying the world's name (this is required if the creator doesn't specify a name).Optionally you can specify that the world should be automatically loaded on server start. See the creator expressions for more information on how to specify the world's name and other settings. If a world with the name (specified or from the creator) already exists, this will just load that world instead of creating a new one.

syntax_3332.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3332.title: Create World
syntax_3332.description: Creates a world with the specified name, optionally with a few settings. Optionally you can specify that the world should be automatically loaded on server start. See the environment type and worldtype type for valid environments and worldtypes respectively. Generator settings can either be custom superflat codes or customized world codes (for customized world codes the worldtype needs to be 'customized'). If a world with the specified name already exists, this will just load that world instead of creating a new one.

en: Creates a world with the specified name, optionally with a few settings. Optionally you can specify that the world should be automatically loaded on server start. See the environment type and worldtype type for valid environments and worldtypes respectively. Generator settings can either be custom superflat codes or customized world codes (for customized world codes the worldtype needs to be 'customized'). If a world with the specified name already exists, this will just load that world instead of creating a new one.

syntax_3333.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3333.title: Delete World
syntax_3333.description: Deletes the specified world. The specified world must be loaded in order to be deleted. There should also not be any players in the specified world when it is deleted.

en: Deletes the specified world. The specified world must be loaded in order to be deleted. There should also not be any players in the specified world when it is deleted.

syntax_3334.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3334.title: Duplicate World
syntax_3334.description: Creates a copy of the specified world using the specified string as a name. The specified world must be loaded in order for this to work.

en: Creates a copy of the specified world using the specified string as a name. The specified world must be loaded in order for this to work.

syntax_3335.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3335.title: Load World Automatically
syntax_3335.description: Tells Kosmos whether it should load the specified world automatically on server start. This is useful for simple and straightforward world management without the need for a world management plugin. Don't run this effect with the main world, as Bukkit will already load that world automatically, and this effect can't be used to enable/disable that behavior.

en: Tells Kosmos whether it should load the specified world automatically on server start. This is useful for simple and straightforward world management without the need for a world management plugin. Don't run this effect with the main world, as Bukkit will already load that world automatically, and this effect can't be used to enable/disable that behavior.

syntax_3336.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3336.title: Unload World
syntax_3336.description: Unloads the specified world. You can specify whether or not to save before unloading (this defaults to true). There should not be any players in the specified world when it is unloaded.

en: Unloads the specified world. You can specify whether or not to save before unloading (this defaults to true). There should not be any players in the specified world when it is unloaded.

syntax_3337.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3337.title: Center of Border
syntax_3337.description: The center of the specified world's border. This isn't necessarily the same as the spawn.

en: The center of the specified world's border. This isn't necessarily the same as the spawn.

syntax_3338.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3338.title: Moving Border Diameter
syntax_3338.description: An expression for a certain property of the moving border of the specified world:original diameter: The diameter of the border when it was last stablefinal diameter: The diameter that the border will be when it stabilizesremaining distance: The distance the border still has to go before it stabilizes
syntax_3338.syntax_pattern:
(original (diameter|side length|size)|(eventual|final) (diameter|side length|size)|remaining distance until [the] border stabilize[s]) of %world%
%world%'s (original (diameter|side length|size)|(eventual|final) (diameter|side length|size)|remaining distance until [the] border stabilize[s])

en: An expression for a certain property of the moving border of the specified world:original diameter: The diameter of the border when it was last stablefinal diameter: The diameter that the border will be when it stabilizesremaining distance: The distance the border still has to go before it stabilizes

syntax_3339.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3339.title: Property of Border
syntax_3339.description:
An expression for a certain property of the specified world's border:
diameter: The diameter/size/side length (Note: you should use 'diameter' instead of 'size' to refer to this property, as 'size' conflicts with Skript's size expression)
damage amount: The amount of damage a player will take per second if they are outside both the border and the damage buffer
damage buffer: The distance (in blocks) outside of the border a player can be before taking damage
warning distance: The distance a player has to be within the border to see the red warning effect
warning time: The amount of time the border should be within of reaching a player to show that player the red warning effect.
syntax_3339.syntax_pattern:
((diameter|side length|size)|damage amount|damage buffer|warning distance|warning time) of %world%
%world%'s ((diameter|side length|size)|damage amount|damage buffer|warning distance|warning time)

en:
An expression for a certain property of the specified world's border:
diameter: The diameter/size/side length (Note: you should use 'diameter' instead of 'size' to refer to this property, as 'size' conflicts with Skript's size expression)
damage amount: The amount of damage a player will take per second if they are outside both the border and the damage buffer
damage buffer: The distance (in blocks) outside of the border a player can be before taking damage
warning distance: The distance a player has to be within the border to see the red warning effect
warning time: The amount of time the border should be within of reaching a player to show that player the red warning effect.

syntax_3340.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3340.title: Time Remaining Until Border Stabilize
syntax_3340.description: An expression for the timespan remaining until the border of the specified world stops moving.
syntax_3340.syntax_pattern:
(time remaining|remaining time) until [the] border stabilize[s] (of|in) %world%
%world%'s (time remaining|remaining time) until [the] border stabilize[s]

en: An expression for the timespan remaining until the border of the specified world stops moving.

syntax_3341.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3341.title: Block in Chunk
syntax_3341.description: The first is the block at the specified coordinates within the specified chunk The second is a center or corner block of a layer, one of the three defaults or a specified number, of the specified chunk.

en: The first is the block at the specified coordinates within the specified chunk The second is a center or corner block of a layer, one of the three defaults or a specified number, of the specified chunk.

syntax_3342.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3342.title: Blocks in Chunk
syntax_3342.description:
The first is all of the blocks in the specified chunk.
The second is the blocks between two coordinates of the specified chunk.
The third is a layer, one of the three defaults or a specified number, of the specified chunk.
The fourth is the layers between two specified numbers of the specified chunk.
syntax_3342.syntax_pattern:
[all] blocks (of|in) %chunk%
blocks [from] %number%, %number%, %number% to %number%, %number%, %number% (of|in) %chunk%
(layer %-number%|top|bottom|sea level) (of|in) %chunk%
[[blocks] from] (layer %-number%|top|bottom|sea level) to (layer %-number%|top|bottom|sea level) (of|in) %chunk%
layers [from] %number% to %number% (of|in) %chunk%

en:
The first is all of the blocks in the specified chunk.
The second is the blocks between two coordinates of the specified chunk.
The third is a layer, one of the three defaults or a specified number, of the specified chunk.
The fourth is the layers between two specified numbers of the specified chunk.

syntax_3343.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3343.title: Chunk at Coordinates
syntax_3343.description:
The first is the chunk at the specified coordinates.
The second is the chunks between the specified coordinates.
The third is the chunk that contains the specified location.
The fourth is the chunks between the specified locations.
syntax_3343.syntax_pattern:
chunk %number%, %number% [in %world%]
chunks [from] %number%, %number% to %number%, %number% [in %world%]
chunk at %location%
chunks from %location% to %location%

en:
The first is the chunk at the specified coordinates.
The second is the chunks between the specified coordinates.
The third is the chunk that contains the specified location.
The fourth is the chunks between the specified locations.

syntax_3344.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3344.title: Coordinate of Chunk
syntax_3344.description: The x or z-coordinate of the specified chunk

en: The x or z-coordinate of the specified chunk

syntax_3345.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3345.title: World of Chunk
syntax_3345.description: The world in which the specified chunk is located

en: The world in which the specified chunk is located

syntax_3346.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3346.title: Biome in Biome Grid
syntax_3346.description: An expression for the biome at the specified x and z coordinates the specified biomegrid.

en: An expression for the biome at the specified x and z coordinates the specified biomegrid.

syntax_3347.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3347.title: Current Chunk Coordinates
syntax_3347.description: An expression, used in the 'generation' section of a custom generator, for a coordinate of the chunk currently being generated

en: An expression, used in the 'generation' section of a custom generator, for a coordinate of the chunk currently being generated

syntax_3348.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3348.title: Material in Chunk Data
syntax_3348.description: An expression for the material at the specified coordinates in the specified chunkdata.

en: An expression for the material at the specified coordinates in the specified chunkdata.

syntax_3349.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3349.title: Difficulty of World
syntax_3349.description: An expression for the difficulty of the specified world. See the Difficulty type for values.

en: An expression for the difficulty of the specified world. See the Difficulty type for values.

syntax_3350.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3350.title: Gamerule
syntax_3350.description: The value (or default value) of the specified gamerule in the specified world. Gamerules whose syntaxes contain the word 'rule' are booleans, while all others are integers (as of Minecraft 1.13.2).
syntax_3350.syntax_pattern:
do tile drops rule [(default)] [in %world%]
do fire tick rule [(default)] [in %world%]
[(default)] max command chain length [in %world%]
reduced debug info rule [(default)] [in %world%]
natural regeneration rule [(default)] [in %world%]
disable elytra movement check rule [(default)] [in %world%]
do mob loot rule [(default)] [in %world%]
announce advancements rule [(default)] [in %world%]
keep inventory rule [(default)] [in %world%]
do entity drops rule [(default)] [in %world%]
do limited crafting rule [(default)] [in %world%]
mob griefing rule [(default)] [in %world%]
[(default)] random tick speed [in %world%]
command block output rule [(default)] [in %world%]
[(default)] spawn radius [in %world%]
do mob spawning rule [(default)] [in %world%]
[(default)] max entity cramming [in %world%]
log admin commands rule [(default)] [in %world%]
spectators generate chunks rule [(default)] [in %world%]
do weather cycle rule [(default)] [in %world%]
send command feedback rule [(default)] [in %world%]
do daylight cycle rule [(default)] [in %world%]
show death messages rule [(default)] [in %world%]

en: The value (or default value) of the specified gamerule in the specified world. Gamerules whose syntaxes contain the word 'rule' are booleans, while all others are integers (as of Minecraft 1.13.2).

syntax_3351.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3351.title: Highest Solid Block
syntax_3351.description: An expression for the highest block that isn't air at a specific location. Useful for setting spawns.
syntax_3351.syntax_pattern:
highest solid block at %location%
highest non-air block at %location%

en: An expression for the highest block that isn't air at a specific location. Useful for setting spawns.

syntax_3352.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3352.title: World from Name
syntax_3352.description: An expression for the world with the specified name.

en: An expression for the world with the specified name.

syntax_3353.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3353.title: Creator of World
syntax_3353.description: An expression for a creator with the properties of the specified world.

en: An expression for a creator with the properties of the specified world.

syntax_3354.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3354.title: Dimension of Creator
syntax_3354.description: An expression for the dimension, or environment, of a creator. This is 'normal' by default.
syntax_3354.syntax_pattern:
[the] dim of %creator%
%creator%'[s] dim
[the] dimension of %creator%
%creator%'[s] dimension
[the] env of %creator%
%creator%'[s] env
[the] environment of %creator%
%creator%'[s] environment

en: An expression for the dimension, or environment, of a creator. This is 'normal' by default.

syntax_3355.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3355.title: Generator of Creator
syntax_3355.description: An expression for the custom generator of a creator. This will not be set if a creator is not specified to use a custom generator.
syntax_3355.syntax_pattern:
[the] gen of %creator%
%creator%'[s] gen
[the] generator of %creator%
%creator%'[s] generator

en: An expression for the custom generator of a creator. This will not be set if a creator is not specified to use a custom generator.

syntax_3356.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3356.title: Generator Settings of Creator
syntax_3356.description: An expression for the generator settings of a creator. This can be a superflat world code or customized world code (the worldtype must be set as superflat and customized respectively in these cases). In addition, a custom generator might also use generator settings.
syntax_3356.syntax_pattern:
[the] gen set of %creator%
%creator%'[s] gen set
[the] generator set of %creator%
%creator%'[s] generator set
[the] gen settings of %creator%
%creator%'[s] gen settings
[the] generator settings of %creator%
%creator%'[s] generator settings

en: An expression for the generator settings of a creator. This can be a superflat world code or customized world code (the worldtype must be set as superflat and customized respectively in these cases). In addition, a custom generator might also use generator settings.

syntax_3357.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3357.title: New Creator
syntax_3357.description: An expression for a creator with the specified name (or no name) and the specified properties. See the WorldCreator type and the Property of WorldCreator expressions for more info.

en: An expression for a creator with the specified name (or no name) and the specified properties. See the WorldCreator type and the Property of WorldCreator expressions for more info.

syntax_3358.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3358.title: Name of Creator
syntax_3358.description: An expression for the worldname of a creator. Not all creators have a set worldname, in which case when creating a world using one you must specify a name.

en: An expression for the worldname of a creator. Not all creators have a set worldname, in which case when creating a world using one you must specify a name.

syntax_3359.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3359.title: Seed of Creator
syntax_3359.description: An expression for the seed of a creator. This can be any string at all instead of having to be a number. This isn't necessarily set, in which case a random seed will be used when creating a world using the creator.

en: An expression for the seed of a creator. This can be any string at all instead of having to be a number. This isn't necessarily set, in which case a random seed will be used when creating a world using the creator.

syntax_3360.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3360.title: Structure Settings of Creator
syntax_3360.description: An condition/expression for whether a creator is set to generate structures.
syntax_3360.syntax_pattern:
[the] struct of %creator%
%creator%'[s] struct
[the] structures of %creator%
%creator%'[s] structures
[the] structure settings of %creator%
%creator%'[s] structure settings

en: An condition/expression for whether a creator is set to generate structures.

syntax_3361.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3361.title: WorldType of Creator
syntax_3361.description: An expression for the worldtype of a creator. This is 'default' by default.

en: An expression for the worldtype of a creator. This is 'default' by default.

syntax_3362.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3362.title: All Automatic Creators
syntax_3362.description: An expression for all of the world creators that Kosmos is currently set to automatically run on server start.

en: An expression for all of the world creators that Kosmos is currently set to automatically run on server start.

syntax_3363.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3363.title: All Current Worlds
syntax_3363.description: An expression for all worlds that are currently loaded. This differs from Skript's 'all worlds' expression in that it still parses as being a list even if there is only one world at the time of parsing.

en: An expression for all worlds that are currently loaded. This differs from Skript's 'all worlds' expression in that it still parses as being a list even if there is only one world at the time of parsing.

syntax_3364.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3364.title: Automatic Creator
syntax_3364.description: An expression for the automatic creator (if there is one) that Kosmos is currently set to run for the world with the specified name.

en: An expression for the automatic creator (if there is one) that Kosmos is currently set to run for the world with the specified name.

syntax_3365.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3365.title: Biome Grid
syntax_3365.description: Represents a 2-dimensional grid of biomes. Used in world generators to manipulate the biome(s) in the chunk to be generated.

en: Represents a 2-dimensional grid of biomes. Used in world generators to manipulate the biome(s) in the chunk to be generated.

syntax_3366.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3366.title: Chunk Data
syntax_3366.description: Represents a 3-dimensional grid of itemstacks. Used in world generators to manipulate the blocks in the chunk to be generated.

en: Represents a 3-dimensional grid of itemstacks. Used in world generators to manipulate the blocks in the chunk to be generated.
The difficulty of a world.

syntax_3368.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3368.title: Dimension
syntax_3368.description: A Minecraft world's dimension, also known as an environment.

en: A Minecraft world's dimension, also known as an environment.

syntax_3369.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3369.title: World Creator
syntax_3369.description: A creator is a way to store data about the various properties of a world for creating it, and can be used in the Create World effect and in Automatic Creator syntax elements to load worlds automatically. See the Property of Creator expressions for more information.

en: A creator is a way to store data about the various properties of a world for creating it, and can be used in the Create World effect and in Automatic Creator syntax elements to load worlds automatically. See the Property of Creator expressions for more information.

syntax_3370.creator: Tlatoani
syntax_3370.title: WorldType
syntax_3370.type_usage: version 1.1 large biomes superflat buffet customized amplified large_biomes version_1_1 flat normal

en: A Minecraft world's world type.
